title: CSV Bulk Import for Inventory on Admin Page

requirements.text: Add a backend function `bulkImportInventory` that accepts an array of inventory item records (partNumber, partName, description, quantity, stockThreshold, category, location). For each record: if the partNumber already exists, update the existing item with the new data; if it does not exist, create a new item. Return a result summary containing counts of items created, updated, and skipped, plus an array of skipped row indices with reasons.

quotes: for duplicates, update the existing item with the new data from the csv being imported
for validation skip the bad and continue the import

acceptanceCriteria: Calling bulkImportInventory with rows where some partNumbers already exist causes those items to be updated with new field values.
Calling bulkImportInventory with rows containing new partNumbers creates those items.
Rows with missing required fields (partNumber, partName, quantity) or non-numeric quantity/stockThreshold are skipped without stopping the import.
The function returns a summary object with createdCount, updatedCount, skippedCount, and a list of skipped row details.

requirements.text: Add a CSV Import section to the AdminPanel page that allows an admin to upload a CSV file with the headers: partNumber, partName, description, quantity, stockThreshold, category, location. Parse the CSV client-side, validate each row (required fields present, quantity and stockThreshold are numeric), skip invalid rows, and call the backend `bulkImportInventory` function with the valid rows. Display a results summary after import showing how many items were created, updated, and skipped, along with details of skipped rows and their reasons.

quotes: add an import feature in the admin page so that a csv file can be uploaded to update the inventory data
for duplicates, update the existing item with the new data from the csv being imported
for validation skip the bad and continue the import

acceptanceCriteria: A file input (accepting .csv) and an Import button are visible on the AdminPanel page for admin users only.
Uploading a valid CSV with correct headers parses all rows and submits them to the backend.
Rows missing required fields or with non-numeric quantity/stockThreshold are skipped client-side before submission.
After import completes, a summary is displayed showing created count, updated count, skipped count, and per-skipped-row details.
The import does not stop when a bad row is encountered; remaining valid rows continue to be processed.
A loading/progress indicator is shown while the import is in progress.
An error message is shown if the uploaded file is not a valid CSV or has incorrect headers.

constraints: CSV headers must match exactly: partNumber, partName, description, quantity, stockThreshold, category, location.
Only admin-role users can access the CSV import feature.
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Importing other data types (users, logs) via CSV.
Server-side CSV file storage or persistence of import history.
